codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata, prior failure review, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff for `92e220a`, and the current `src/model/stacking.py`, `src/model/calibration.py`, and `tests/test_stacking.py` code paths. This fix resolves the reviewed gate defect from `t18-lr-stacking`: the default stacking path now carries `enforce_holdout_brier_gate=True`, `_train_single_stacking_model()` only persists artifacts when `stacked_brier <= base_brier`, both ML and RL default-path regressions are covered by a dedicated test, and calibration explicitly opts out of the gate for its reduced-search internal training path so calibration bundle creation still works. I did not find any new blocking issues within this fix's scope.

summary: Pass. The fix feature adequately closes the prior artifact-persistence gate failure by preventing default-path ML and RL stacking artifacts from being written when holdout Brier regresses versus the base XGBoost model, while preserving the calibration workflow via an explicit gate bypass in `src/model/calibration.py`. No new in-scope blocking problems were identified during review.
